commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Update Supabase client to read from window.ENV runtime config

- Modify supabaseClient.js to prioritize window.ENV over import.meta.env
- This allows Supabase config to work with existing env/env.*.js system
- Add fallback to import.meta.env for development flexibility
- Create .env.local.example for alternative Vite-based config
- Update warning message to mention both config options

Issue: Supabase client was only reading import.meta.env (Vite build-time),
but our project uses env/env.*.js with window.ENV (runtime config)

Solution: Check window.ENV first, then fall back to import.meta.env

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(clippy): suppress aarch64 NEON clippy warnings in weight dequant (#3300)

## What changed
Added file-level clippy allow attributes to neon_weight_dequant.rs and
neon_weight_dequant_v2.rs for manual_is_multiple_of,
needless_range_loop, and related lints.

## Why
macOS ARM64 Clippy job fails with 129 errors on every PR. These files
are aarch64-gated so errors only appear on ARM runners. The pattern is
already established in other NEON kernel files.

## What was tested
- cargo clippy on x86 passes cleanly
- CI will validate on macOS ARM64

## Risks
None - style lints in unsafe NEON intrinsic code.

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing DELETE RLS policy for beneficiario table

Beneficiarios were not being deleted because no DELETE policy existed.
Added benef_delete policy for admin/supervisor. Also added 0-row
delete detection, success message, and form close after delete.

IMPORTANT: Run this SQL in Supabase SQL Editor:
  CREATE POLICY benef_delete ON beneficiario
  FOR DELETE USING (is_admin_or_supervisor());

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: thread-safe group_id read before pause-on-BT-disconnect

Status dict is shared across asyncio and daemon reader threads.
Read group_id under _status_lock to avoid stale-read race condition.
Applies to both _monitor_polling and _monitor_dbus disconnect paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 67 game telemetry integration tests

Add 67 integration tests covering game-specific telemetry parsing,
adapter registration/discovery, packet routing, config generation,
game auto-detection, multi-game concurrency, rate limiting, data
invariants, cross-game normalization consistency, and error handling.

Games tested include Forza Motorsport (UDP binary), Live for Speed
(OutGauge UDP), Rennsport (binary UDP), WRC Generations (Codemasters
Mode 1 UDP), SimHub (JSON UDP), and MudRunner (JSON UDP).

All tests use Result-returning patterns (no unwrap/expect) and
validate adapter behavior with realistic packet formats.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 84 watchdog and hardware watchdog tests

* test: comprehensive watchdog and safety interlock hardening tests

Add watchdog_hardening_tests.rs (openracing-watchdog) with 40+ tests:
- Feed timing accuracy (boundary, average, zero-time)
- Timeout triggering (threshold, streak interruption, HID independence)
- Reset/restart lifecycle (fresh cycles, re-registration)
- Multi-channel coordination (isolation, callbacks, metrics)
- Platform-specific behavior (cfg-gated OS tests)
- Concurrent load (write/read, heartbeats, health checks, deadlock)
- Safety state machine interaction (failure progression, fault types)

Add hw_watchdog_tests.rs (openracing-hardware-watchdog) with 44 tests:
- Mock HardwareWatchdog trait operations (full lifecycle)
- Timeout configuration (boundary values, builder validation)
- Error injection (timeout injection, safe state, error messages)
- Exhaustive state machine transitions (valid + invalid)
- Concurrent access (mutex-based, atomic state)
- Metrics tracking (feed intervals, success rate, saturation)

All tests use Result-returning signatures; no unwrap()/expect().

commit.message:
test: add 63 anticheat and audit crypto tests

Add anticheat_hardening_tests.rs with comprehensive test coverage for:

- HMAC-SHA256 correctness against RFC 4231 test vectors (6 cases)
- HMAC-SHA256 edge cases (empty key, block-size key, determinism)
- Audit log entry creation and signing with HMAC-SHA256
- Audit log chain verification (valid chains, wrong key)
- Tamper detection: modified payload, event type, HMAC, sequence,
  timestamp, missing entries, reordered entries, inserted entries
- Audit log rotation with tail hash preservation
- Large chain verification (1000 entries)
- Concurrent audit log access (8 threads × 50 events)
- Anticheat report initialization, serialization roundtrip
- Anticheat report markdown generation and content verification
- Anticheat state machine transitions (valid and invalid)
- Platform-specific anticheat behavior (DLL injection, kernel drivers)
- Game detection integration (telemetry compatibility assertions)
- SHA256 known test vectors
- JSON serialization roundtrip for audit entries
- Unicode and special character payloads

All tests use Result-returning pattern (no unwrap/expect).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial release: LabelMe Augmentor v2.0.0

Advanced data augmentation tool for LabelMe annotated datasets with comprehensive
validation and per-class configuration support.

Key Features:
- 40+ Albumentations transforms with mask support
- Per-class augmentation strategies and validation rules
- 9 validation parameters (area, length, shape, border, preservation)
- Angle-independent defect length measurement
- Relative area preservation (works with resize transforms)
- Parallel processing with checkpoint/resume support
- Debug visualizations and comprehensive logging

Validation Parameters:
- Area: min/max_defect_area
- Length: min/max_defect_length (NEW - rotated bounding box)
- Shape: min_contour_points
- Preservation: check_defect_preservation, max_area_change_ratio
- Border: reject_border_defects, border_margin

Technical Highlights:
- Pydantic-based configuration with schema validation
- Type-safe implementation throughout
- 52 unit tests (100% pass rate)
- Compatible with Python 3.8-3.11
- MIT License

Author: Jakhongir Nodirov <jakhon37@gmail.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add 22 burnout exercise modals (orange theme, phases 0-4)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: scalability improvements for multi-device deployments

- SSE event batching (100ms window) in state.py: prevent notification
  storm when many devices update status simultaneously; reduces SSE
  events by ~10x under mass-reconnect scenarios
- Explicit ThreadPoolExecutor sized to device count (min 64 workers)
  prevents BT reconnect queue starvation at 100+ devices
- D-Bus MessageBus reuse across BT reconnect iterations: no longer
  creates/destroys a new bus connection on every outer loop iteration;
  reconnects only when bus is unresponsive
- Keepalive silence: random startup jitter (0..interval) prevents
  simultaneous paplay bursts across all devices
- _status_monitor_loop sleep increased 2s -> 5s (reduces asyncio
  wakeups from 50/s to 20/s at 100 devices; disconnect still caught
  instantly via D-Bus signals)
- WEB_THREADS env var: configurable Waitress thread count (default 4,
  recommend 16 for deployments with 20+ devices)
- Group-aware pause before BT disconnect stop: solo players receive
  pause signal before sendspin stops; grouped players skip it
- fix: restore missing async def stop_sendspin() declaration
- fix: guard proc.stdin None before write in keepalive burst

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Institutional-grade upgrade: 5-phase signal quality, reliability, and transparency overhaul (#29)

* Initial plan

* feat: comprehensive upgrade — new modules, confluence scoring, session filter, BTC correlation

Part 1 — New bot modules:
- bot/structure_detector.py: fractal swing point detection, dealing range, liquidity levels
- bot/btc_correlation.py: BTC macro bias gate for altcoin signals
- bot/session_filter.py: London/NYC session filter (07-16 and 12-21 UTC)
- bot/vwap.py: VWAP calculator with proximity check
- bot/confluence_score.py: weighted 0-100 confluence scoring across all factors
- bot/chart_generator.py: mplfinance annotated signal chart (graceful fallback)
- bot/dashboard_web.py: Flask performance dashboard with /dashboard, /api/stats, /api/signals
- bot/signal_tracker.py: thread-safe signal lifecycle tracker (TP/SL/BE events)

Part 2 — Existing file modifications:
- bot/logging_config.py: use secrets.token_hex(6) instead of random for signal IDs
- bot/signal_engine.py: add confluence_score field, confidence stars in format_message,
  Bybit copy-trade format, updated confidence logic (FVG+OB+4H direction check)
- bot/ws_manager.py: add 15m candle buffer/stream (backward-compatible has_sufficient_data)

xpc: implement connection interruption handling with auto-reconnect (#529)

Handle NSXPCConnection interruption and invalidation with automatic
reconnection, exponential backoff, and operation queuing.

- Add XPCConnectionManager actor with state tracking (connected,
  interrupted, reconnecting, invalidated)
- Exponential backoff reconnection with configurable parameters
- Operation queuing during reconnect with max queue size
- ServiceProxy wrapper for safe cross-isolation transfer
- XPCConnectionMonitor protocol for state change observation
- Wire connection manager into FileProvider extension
- 16 new tests covering interruption, reconnection, invalidation,
  queue overflow, and backoff behavior

  SDK to create/update the iq-engine-index idempotently. Schema: 19

* fix: correct text-embedding-3-large vector dimensions 1536→3072

text-embedding-3-large outputs 3072-dim vectors by default.
1536 is text-embedding-ada-002. Without an explicit dimensions
parameter in the API call, the index would fail with a dimension
mismatch on every document upload.

- src/ingestion/index_manager.py: _VECTOR_DIMENSIONS 1536→3072
- src/ingestion/embedder.py: _EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS 1536→3072 + docstrings
- src/engine/model_client.py: stale 1536-dim comment → 3072-dim
- infra/search_index.tf: dimensions=1536→3072, fix ada-002 comment
- CHANGELOG.md: document fix

Co-authored-by: johnstel <9366764+johnstel@users.noreply.github.com>

minor-fixes: infer update channel from binary version, block PR builds (#7011)

Two fixes for azd update:

1. Channel auto-detection: LoadUpdateConfig() defaults to ChannelStable
when no config exists, causing daily builds to check the wrong update
source and display 'stable channel'. Add inferChannelFromVersion() that
detects 'daily.' in the binary version string. Both azd update and the
background auto-update check now use the correct channel by default.
Explicit config still takes precedence.

2. Block PR builds: Replace IsDevVersion() with IsNonProdVersion() guard
in update.go and main.go so both dev builds (0.0.0-dev.0) and PR builds
(1.24.0-beta.1-pr.XXXXX) are blocked from self-updating.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Improve number line visualization and add random challenges to Baker's Challenge

- Encourage mental math and fraction visualization instead of reading labels

- Dynamically generate available buttons based on target measurement
- Add 'Next Challenge' button to cycle through different recipes
